adjudication_raw:
Review: Document AI startup receipt extraction model vs. commercial OCR baseline.

Verdict: empirical_test_agreed

The study has two configuration issues that compromise the accuracy gap as a measure of model quality:

1. The commercial vendor was run with its default general-document configuration. The vendor offers a receipt-specific enterprise parser that was not used. For a receipt extraction evaluation, this is the purpose-built configuration.

2. 30% of the evaluation set contains bilingual English-French Canadian receipts with province-specific tax formats. The startup fine-tuned on bilingual data; the vendor ran with English-only settings. The vendor is systematically disadvantaged on 30% of test cases through a configuration choice unrelated to model quality.

These issues are genuine but correctable — the vendor could be re-evaluated with receipt-specific + bilingual settings on the same dataset. The startup's specialization gives reason to believe a genuine advantage may persist. The question is whether the 8.4 pp gap holds under a fair comparison.

Empirical test: measure exact-field accuracy with vendor in receipt-specific enterprise mode and bilingual settings. Success criterion: startup leads by ≥ 5 pp. Failure criterion: gap falls below 5 pp or disappears.

empirical_test.measure: Exact-field accuracy on the 8,400-receipt evaluation set
empirical_test.success_criterion: Startup model leads vendor (receipt-specific parser, bilingual mode) by ≥ 5 pp
empirical_test.failure_criterion: Startup lead falls below 5 pp with vendor in optimal configuration
